title: Render initials only on token-accessed profile and match history views
description: The /my-profile/{token} and /my-profile/{token}/history URLs are effectively shareable links (forwarded in chats, screenshots). Full first+last names are currently rendered on these pages (profile.html lines 349-358, match_history.html line 156), leaking the player's identity to anyone with the URL. Match the existing availability page pattern and render initials only (e.g. 'J.H.') on all templates served under /my-profile/*.
acceptance_criteria: templates/players/profile.html renders '{{printf "%.1s" .Player.FirstName}}.{{printf "%.1s" .Player.LastName}}.' in the hero position instead of the full name
PreferredName is not rendered on profile.html (preferred names may still reveal identity)
templates/players/match_history.html renders the same initials pattern wherever the player is named
Any other template reachable under /my-profile/* also renders initials only
Page <title> on /my-profile/* routes uses a generic title (e.g. 'My Tennis') rather than the player's name
Opposing players / teammates rendered on these pages (e.g. match history opponents) are also rendered with initials — no third-party identity leak
The availability page continues to render initials the same way (no regression)

do_not_change: Server-side data loading — we still load the full Player into template context; we just do not render the name

testing_requirements: Manual: open /my-profile/{token} and confirm no first/last/preferred name appears in the rendered HTML
Manual: open /my-profile/{token}/history and confirm initials-only for player and opponents

context.user_story: As a player, I want my profile page to hide my full name even if someone forwards my shareable link, because the URL is effectively public.
